overview: Jeskai Control in Modern relies on a suite of permanent-based answers and repeatable board resets to outlast aggressive and midrange decks. By leveraging Narset, Parter of Veils for early board control and Teferi, Time Raveler for late-game acceleration, the deck establishes a resilient board state. Orim's Chant serves as a flexible, repeatable reset button to clear threats and protect key assets.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents with superior card advantage from Narset, Parter of Veils
Stabilizing with Teferi, Time Raveler to untap and draw cards while denying opponent's turns
Clearing the board with Orim's Chant to reset the game state in your favor

keep_criteria: Hands with early interaction or mana dorks
Hands containing Narset or Teferi with sufficient lands
Hands with Orim's Chant and removal
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking lands or early play
Hands with only late-game permanents and no answers
Hands without card draw or board presence

early_game: Play lands and cheap interaction to survive early pressure
Prioritize Narset, Parter of Veils for immediate board control and card draw
Hold up mana for Orim's Chant if facing aggressive starts
mid_game: Deploy Teferi, Time Raveler to establish a tempo advantage
Use Orim's Chant to clear wide boards or protect key permanents
Maintain board presence while cycling through answers
late_game: Leverage Narset's draw engine to exhaust opponent's resources
Use Teferi's untap ability to accelerate mana and draw
Cast Orim's Chant repeatedly to maintain board control until victory

key_cards.name: Teferi, Time Raveler
key_cards.role: Late-game stabilizer and card advantage engine
key_cards.notes: Activate untap ability to draw cards and accelerate mana; use -1 to exile threats and protect Narset | Played early to establish a +1/+1 land drop engine, crucial for stabilizing against Nadu's explosive starts and enabling consistent mana for sweepers. (from gameplay) | Its +1 ability grants flash to sorceries, enabling instant-speed board wipes. Also benefits from vigilance via Mamo, Ancient Castle to survive combat. (from gameplay)

key_cards.name: Orim's Chant
key_cards.role: Flexible board wipe and protection spell
key_cards.notes: Cast on opponent's turn to clear threats, or on your turn to protect Narset and Teferi from removal | Used to lock down the opponent's mana and draw cards, but requires protection against Nadu's uncounterable threats. (from gameplay)

key_cards.name: Narset, Parter of Veils
key_cards.role: Early-game board control and card draw
key_cards.notes: Play early to exile threats and draw cards; keep protected by Orim's Chant or Teferi's exile ability

sequencing_tips: Hold up Orim's Chant for opponent's turn to maximize board clearance
Prioritize Narset early to establish card advantage before deploying Teferi
Use Teferi's untap ability to cast Orim's Chant or play additional permanents
Sequence removal to protect Narset and Teferi from targeted hate
Bank energy early with 1-mana spells (Galvanic Discharge, Tune the Narrative) to cast Wrath of the Skies for 2 mana, leaving mana open for instant-speed interaction.
When facing Nadu Winged Wisdom, only use Dress Down as a delay tactic if you have a follow-up removal spell ready for the next turn; otherwise, remove it immediately.
Use Subtlety to exile uncounterable threats to the top of the library, stalling their resolution for a turn to resolve your own sweepers.
Use Teferi, Time Raveler's +1 ability to grant flash to sorceries, allowing the new sweeper to be cast instant-speed to clear boards before attacks or resolves.
When casting the new sweeper, do not auto-reveal the X cost for energy. Wait for the opponent to declare responses first to avoid giving away strategic information.
Prioritize keeping removal in hand to protect Teferi, Time Raveler rather than bouncing an enchanted threat if it would allow the opponent to attack with multiple creatures that would kill Teferi.

matchups.advice: Prioritize Narset and Orim's Chant to clear early threats; hold up mana for interaction
Establish Narset early to outvalue; use Teferi to control the board and draw
Disrupt early plays with interaction; use Orim's Chant to clear combo pieces

Wrath of the Skies is the premier answer to Bant Nadu's multi-pronged threats (Shuko, Urza's Saga, Nadu) because it clears creatures, artifacts, and enchantments simultaneously. (from gameplay) Bant Nadu will often test waters by casting Summoner's Pact in response to Urza's Saga to gauge your counter spells before committing to the full Shuko combo; keep interaction up to disrupt this. (from gameplay) The new sweeper is a critical tool against Bant Nadu, efficiently clearing early threats and hitting enchantments in land form to severely delay their rebuild. (from gameplay) Keep extra blue cards in hand during discard phases to fuel cheap counterspells against Nadu's aggressive curve and mana acceleration. (from gameplay) Use the energy removal spell to target Nadu's mana creatures and bounce them to the top of the graveyard, disrupting their mana base while dealing damage. (from gameplay)
